title: Xeriscent Comforting Algerian Rice Pilaf
description: This Algerian rice pilaf is cooked with traditional techniques, resulting in a chewy, comforting dish.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the cumin and garam masala into a fine powder.
Add the ground spices to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is lightly toasted.
Add the rice wine and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan.
Simmer for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and garnish with chopped parsley, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
